title: carya ovalis var. mexicana
intro: Carya ovalis var. mexicana, also known as the Mexican hickory, is a deciduous tree species within the Carya genus, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This tree has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its bark, which contains tannins and other compounds that exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Carya ovalis var. mexicana include treating various skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the bark of this tree is often dried and made into teas, tinctures, or infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
